action.hypothesis: Top-k was only modestly competitive and still far from the K=128 quality target, so the next fundamentally different probe is the gated SAE family using only launcher-safe overrides; separating support selection from magnitude may recover cleaner latent routing than both JumpReLU and plain top-k.
action.summary: Launch a proxy gated-family prototype at K=128 using the strongest validated training regime, but avoid architecture-specific override keys that previously caused invalid-env crashes.
action.self_review: This keeps the round focused on a real architecture change rather than another exhausted local sweep, and it directly addresses the recent gated launch failures by using only confirmed-safe env overrides. The main risk is that gated remains worse than top-k and JumpReLU, but that is the most informative remaining architecture probe available without code changes.
notes_to_memory: Top-k produced a clean proxy run but remained far above the current full K=128 JumpReLU frontier, so a stronger architecture change is still needed.
Recent gated crashes were caused by invalid env overrides rather than confirmed model failure, so the family deserves one clean retry with only generic override keys.
This round tests whether gated routing can provide a more meaningful architecture departure than plain top-k while keeping the optimization regime fixed.
next_hypotheses: If gated runs cleanly and improves proxy FVU, stabilize the family with a follow-up that tunes gate-specific parameters or promotes to full if the gain is large enough.
If gated runs cleanly but is still uncompetitive, conclude that the current non-code architecture set is exhausted and plan a code-backed new family next.

If gated still crashes under launcher-safe overrides, retire it as an execution-layer mismatch and focus future architecture exploration on families that can be expressed with the current valid override surface.
